questions.original_question: LTIC向Geely UK Limited转让LTIC股权的转让对价是多少？
questions.answer:
关于LTIC股份从LTIC转让给Geely UK Limited的考虑，根据2024年10月1日发布的信息，Lotus Technology Innovative Limited（LTIL）与Geely UK Limited签署了股份转让协议，LTIL同意出售80万LTIC股份给Geely UK Limited，交易金额为1088万欧元。此次交易完成后，Geely UK Limited将持有LTIC 80%的股份。这一交易进一步巩固了Geely在路特斯科技中的战略布局，并支持其在全球智能豪华出行领域的发展。

以上信息基于2024年10月1日之前的知识。
rewritten_question: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: Under the terms of the agreement, Lotus Technology received a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and non-sublicensable license for the electric automotive chassis and autonomous driving technology platform, referred to as the “Geely License,” with a consideration of US$288.9 million. The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain of Lotus Technology's R&D projects. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022. The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology purchased a one-year convertible note with a principal amount of US$10.0 million issued by ECARX Holding Inc. Upon the listing of ECARX Holding Inc. on December 21, 2022, the note was automatically converted into Class A ordinary shares of ECARX Holding Inc. at a conversion price of US$9.50 per share. As of December 31, 2023 and 2022, the fair value of such shares was US$3.3 million and US$8.4 million, respectively. On June 24, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into the CB Purchase Agreement with Geely HK, pursuant to which Geely HK purchased from Lotus Technology the Geely Convertible Bond in two tranches in an aggregate principal amount equal to the U.S. dollar equivalent of RMB800 million through a private placement.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

rag_info.chunk_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a Put Option Agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions. See “Agreements Entered Into in Connection with the Business Combination    —     Put Option Agreements.” Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into the Put Option Agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right, but not the obligation, to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on Lotus Group International Limited’s total revenue for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: Each of the put options granted to Geely and Etika may only be exercised during the period from April 1 to June 30, 2025, and is contingent upon satisfaction of the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries exceeds 5,000 in 2024. The put options granted to Geely and Etika can be exercised independently from each other, and the exercise of each put option is not conditioned upon the exercise of the other.

rag_info.chunk_content: Pursuant to the terms of the agreements, if the consummation of the Business Combination occurs prior to the maturity date, all of the then outstanding principal amount and any accrued but unpaid interest (as applicable) of the notes will be automatically converted into LTC Ordinary Shares upon the consummation of the Business Combination. In January 2023, Lotus Technology issued put options to each of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely HK”) and Etika at nil consideration. Pursuant to the put option agreements, each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of LGIL, between April 1, 2025 and June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price. The exercise price will be settled with LTC Ordinary Shares. In November 2022, the former VIE’s subsidiary, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. (“Lightning Speed”), entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Lightning Speed is entitled to issue exchangeable notes to obtain financing from the investor. Each tranche of the exchangeable notes is scheduled to mature on the five-year anniversary date of issuance. With the consent of the investor, each tranche of the exchangeable notes is eligible for two extensions, and each extension shall not exceed one year.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the consideration for the transfer of LTIC shares from LTIC to Geely UK Limited?

rag_info.chunk_content: On January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology issued put options to each of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely HK”) and Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd (“Etika”) at nil consideration. Pursuant to the put option agreements, each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of LGIL, during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price, i.e. 1.15 multiplied by the revenue of LGIL for the year ending December 31, 2024 plus the cash and cash equivalents of LGIL as of December 31, 2024, and minus the outstanding amount of indebtedness of LGIL as of December 31, 2024 (the “exercise price”), if the total number of vehicles sold by LGIL in 2024 exceeds 5,000 (the “exercise condition”). Geely HK and Etika are entitled to exercise the put option on an individual standalone basis. If

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) is a world-class engineering facility in electric mobility. As a part of Lotus Technology's international research and development network, LTIC develops new products and solutions for a new era of premium performance driving and participates in the development of Lotus Technology's high-end technology, such as regional development for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), Electric Powertrain Architecture (EPA), new architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin systems. Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) adopts an agile organizational structure where its members can work in different working teams based on the focuses of various phases of the project. LTIC works in three main areas: • The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. • The regional development and delivery team collaborates with the U.K. and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for ensuring compliance of Lotus Technology's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters. • The innovation and new platform development team is responsible for creating new concepts and new architectures for Lotus Technology's future product pipeline. This team studies new technologies and pioneers advancements in digital vehicle dynamics.

The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. metadata.title_summary:
title: LTIC Share Transfer Agreement 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the LTIC Share Transfer Agreement**

Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., has executed a Share Transfer Agreement with Geely UK Limited (Geely) for the sale of 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (LTIC), a German company that is also a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL. The total transaction amount for this share transfer is EUR 10,880,000. 

The transaction was finalized on September 27, 2024, coinciding with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. As a result of this transaction, Geely now possesses 80% of LTIC's share capital. The details of the Share Transfer Agreement are documented in this report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the description provided here is subject to the complete terms outlined in that exhibit.

This agreement marks a significant strategic move for both LTIL and Geely, reflecting Geely's increased stake in LTIC and reinforcing its position in the market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC finalized a put option agreement with Geely and Etika, complementing the Original Merger Agreement. This arrangement allows Geely and Etika to compel LTC to buy their equity stakes in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price will be based on LGIL's total revenue for 2024, adjusted for net debt, and the transaction can take place between April 1, 2025, and June 30, 2025, provided specific pre-agreed conditions are met.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrent with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into put option agreements with Geely and Etika as part of the Original Merger Agreement. These agreements provide Geely and Etika with the right to compel LTC to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, which is the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price for these equity interests will be determined based on the total revenue of LGIL for the fiscal year 2024, with adjustments made to exclude net debt. The options can be exercised within a specified timeframe, from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and their execution is contingent upon the fulfillment of certain pre-agreed conditions. This arrangement is designed to ensure that both Geely and Etika have a clear exit strategy while aligning their interests with LTC's performance in the upcoming fiscal year.

The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain of Lotus Technology's R&D projects. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. metadata.title_summary:
title: Related Party Transactions 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions

The "Related Party Transactions" chapter outlines the company's extensive financial interactions with related parties over the years, particularly focusing on the sale of sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and consulting services. 

#### Revenue from Related Party Transactions
- **Service Revenue**: The company generated revenue from services provided to related parties amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$12.6 million
  - **2022**: US$8.3 million
  - **2021**: US$3.3 million
- **Product Sales**: Sales of products to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$3.4 million
  - **2022**: US$23 thousand
  - **2021**: None

#### Accounts Receivable and Payments
- As of December 31, 2023, accounts receivable from related parties totaled **US$22.4 million**, up from **US$8.5 million** in 2022.
- The company made payments on behalf of related parties totaling:
  - **2023**: US$4.0 million
  - **2022**: US$244 thousand
  - **2021**: None
- Related parties collected **US$3.7 million** in 2023.

#### Loans and Prepayments
- Unsecured short-term loans to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$1.2 million
  - **2022**: US$2.3 million
- Interest income from these loans was recognized at:
  - **2023**: US$122 thousand
  - **2022**: US$24 thousand
- Prepayments for vehicle purchases to related parties amounted to:
  - **2023**: US$21.4 million
  - **2022**: US$6.1 million
- An expected credit loss provision for these prepayments increased from **US$12 thousand** on January 1, 2023, to **US$23 thousand** by December 31, 2023.

#### Purchases from Related Parties
- Total purchases from related parties were significant, amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$852.6 million (with **US$549.9 million** recognized as cost of goods sold)
  - **2022**: US$44.3 million
  - **2021**: US$14.3 million
- Research and development expenses related to these purchases were:
  - **2023**: US$134.7 million
  - **2022**: US$167.0 million
  - **2021**: US$47.4 million
- Equipment and software purchases from related parties totaled **US$11.2 million** in 2023 and **US$42.8 million** in 2022.
- The company incurred short-term lease costs of **US$765 thousand** in 2023.

#### Agreements and Investments
- In 2023, the company established a distribution agreement with Lotus Cars Limited, recording a payable of **US$23.0 million** for distribution rights.
- The company had previously entered into a license agreement for the Geely License, expensing **US$288.9 million** as R&D costs in 2021.
- Additionally, equity interests were acquired in related entities, including a **US$15.5 million** transaction with Geely UK Limited completed in June 2022.
- The fair value of a convertible note purchased from ECARX Holding Inc. was **US$3.3 million** as of December 31, 2023.

Overall, the transactions with related parties reflect significant financial interactions that have notably impacted both revenues and expenses across multiple years, underscoring the importance of these relationships in the company's financial landscape.

The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain R&D projects of Lotus Group. Lotus Group concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. (viii) On December 2, 2021, Lotus Group, through its subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at the consideration of US\$15,512, which was settled in June 2022.

metadata.title_summary:
title: NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions and Financial Obligations

As of December 31, 2023, the Group has engaged in various related party transactions and financial obligations that significantly impact its consolidated and combined financial statements. Below is a detailed summary of these transactions, categorized by type and including relevant financial figures.

#### 1. Accounts Payable and Current Liabilities

**Purchases from Related Parties:**
- The Group's purchases from related parties, which include sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and consulting services, have increased substantially:
  - **2023:** US$852,623
  - **2022:** US$44,347
  - **2021:** US$14,259
- These purchases were recognized as cost of goods sold:
  - **2023:** US$549,893
  - **2022:** US$839
  - **2021:** US$331
- As of December 31, inventories recorded were:
  - **2023:** US$248,331
  - **2022:** US$21,880
- Accrued expenses included amounts due for office materials and services:
  - **2023:** US$27,660
  - **2022:** US$8,684
- Accounts payable for these purchases stood at:
  - **2023:** US$340,419
  - **2022:** US$5,770

**Research and Development (R&D) Expenses:**
- R&D expenses from related parties were as follows:
  - **2023:** US$134,721
  - **2022:** US$167,012
  - **2021:** US$47,442
- Amounts due for R&D included in accrued expenses:
  - **2023:** US$217,256
  - **2022:** US$162,942

**Equipment and Software Purchases:**
- Purchases from related parties for equipment and software were recorded as:
  - **2023:** US$11,223
  - **2022:** US$42,798
  - **2021:** US$6,255
- Accrued expenses included amounts due:
  - **2023:** US$19,786
  - **2022:** US$8,366

**Miscellaneous Expenses:**
- Related parties incurred various expenses totaling US$14,514 and US$2,952, with amounts due included in accrued expenses:
  - **2023:** US$1,605
  - **2022:** US$2,952

**Short-term Leases:**
- Short-term lease costs incurred were:
  - **2023:** US$765
  - **2022:** US$347
  - **2021:** US$243
- Payables for short-term leases included in accrued expenses:
  - **2023:** US$323
  - **2022:** US$272

**Deposits from Related Parties:**
- Deposits received from related parties were:
  - **2023:** US$251
  - **2022:** US$1,584
- These amounts are included in accrued expenses and other non-current liabilities.

#### 2. Significant Agreements and Transactions

**Distribution Agreement:**
- On January 31, 2023, the Group entered into a distribution agreement with LCL for the exclusive global distribution of certain vehicle models, with a payable of US$23,047 included in accrued expenses.

**Loan and Interest:**
- A one-year unsecured loan of US$10,211 was borrowed from a related party in 2019 and repaid in August 2022, with interest expenses recorded for 2022 and 2021.

**Lease Agreements:**
- Right-of-use assets recognized from lease agreements were:
  - **2023:** US$12,166
  - **2022:** US$214
  - **2021:** US$1,333
- Lease liabilities paid were:
  - **2023:** US$269
  - **2022:** US$98
  - **2021:** US$545

**License Agreement:**
- A license agreement with Zhejiang Liankong was established on March 12, 2021, for electric automotive technology at a cost of US$288,948, expensed as R&D.

**Trademark Licenses:**
- Trademark licenses were obtained from Group Lotus Limited in November 2021 through share issuance.

**Equity Transfer Agreement:**
- The Group acquired a 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from Geely UK Limited for US$15,512, settled in June 2022.

**Convertible Note Purchase:**
- A convertible note of US$10,000 was purchased from Ecarx, which was converted to shares upon listing. Fair values recorded were US$3,326 as of December 31, 2023, and US$8,411 as of December 31, 2022.

**Manufacturing Agreement:**
- A manufacturing agreement with Geely Auto for electric vehicle production commenced on June 21, 2022, outlining responsibilities for design, production, and quality control.

This summary provides a comprehensive overview of the Group's related party transactions and financial obligations, highlighting significant figures and agreements that shape its financial landscape as of December 31, 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Liquidity and Capital Resources 
summary: The summary of cash flows and working capital provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance across various periods, emphasizing cash inflows and outflows, liquidity trends, and operational efficiency. The data covers two six-month periods ending June 30 for 2023 and 2022, as well as full years ending December 31 for 2022 and 2021, detailing key metrics in operating, investing, and financing activities, along with the effects of exchange rate changes.

Key findings indicate a notable increase in net cash used in operating activities, with losses escalating significantly in both the six-month and full-year comparisons. In contrast, cash outflows from investing activities showed a decrease in the latest six-month period, while the previous full year experienced a shift from positive inflows to negative outflows. Financing activities, however, provided substantial cash inflows, reflecting a strong reliance on financing to support operations.

The impact of exchange rate fluctuations was detrimental, contributing to losses in cash balances. The company's cash position revealed a decline in net cash and restricted cash in 2023 compared to the previous year, despite an increase in opening cash balances. The company has historically funded its operations through various financing means, including convertible notes and capital contributions, but now faces challenges in securing additional liquidity for ongoing operations.

To address these challenges, the company is exploring multiple strategies for securing funding, including potential external financing linked to a Business Combination and issuing new convertible notes. However, there is uncertainty regarding the success of these strategies and the company's ability to continue operations amid ongoing profitability issues and negative cash flows.

Additionally, the company has entered into various agreements related to convertible notes and exchangeable notes, which may impact future financing needs and operational strategies. The company generates revenue in RMB and anticipates ongoing challenges related to foreign currency conversion and regulatory approvals, which could further complicate its financial stability.

In summary, while the company has a strong cash position relative to previous years, it faces significant cash flow challenges, increased operating losses, and reliance on financing, necessitating strategic improvements and liquidity management to ensure future operational viability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into a Put Option Agreement with Geely and Etika in conjunction with the Original Merger Agreement. Pursuant to the put option agreements, each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of LGIL, during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price, i.e. 1.15 multiplied by the revenue of LGIL for the year ending December 31, 2024 plus the cash and cash equivalents of LGIL as of December 31, 2024, and minus the outstanding amount of indebtedness of LGIL as of December 31, 2024 (the “exercise price”), if the total number of vehicles sold by LGIL in 2024 exceeds 5,000 (the “exercise condition”). Geely HK and Etika are entitled to exercise the put option on an individual standalone basis. If

metadata.title_summary:
title: 13.   PUT OPTION LIABILITIES 
summary: The "Put Option Liabilities Reconciliation" provides an overview of the changes in put option liabilities from December 31, 2022, to June 30, 2023. It highlights the issuance of put options, their fair value fluctuations, and the final balance as of June 30, 2023, all in US dollars. 

The reconciliation is based on put option agreements established on January 31, 2023, with Geely HK and Etika, which grant these entities the right to sell their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd back to the Company, contingent on LGIL's sales performance in 2024. 

Key details include an initial balance of zero for put option liabilities at the end of 2022, the issuance of options valued at US$9,376 on January 31, 2023, and a subsequent decrease in fair values amounting to US$(3,307) by June 30, 2023. This resulted in a final balance of US$6,069 in put option liabilities. 

The options, granted at no cost, can be exercised between April 1, 2025, and June 30, 2025, provided LGIL sells over 5,000 vehicles in 2024. The exercise price is determined by a formula based on LGIL's revenue, cash reserves, and outstanding debts as of December 31, 2024. Both Geely HK and Etika have the ability to exercise their options independently.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Each of the put options granted to Geely and Etika may only be exercised during the period from April 1 to June 30, 2025, and is contingent upon satisfaction of the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries exceeds 5,000 in 2024. The put options granted to Geely and Etika can be exercised independently from each other, and the exercise of each put option is not conditioned upon the exercise of the other.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: LTIC is a world-class engineering facility in electric mobility. As a part of Lotus Technology's international research and development network, LTIC develops new products and solutions for a new era of premium performance driving and participates in the development of Lotus Technology's high-end technology, such as regional development for advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), electric powertrain architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC adopts an agile organizational structure where its members can work in different teams based on the focuses of various phases of the project. LTIC operates in three main areas: ● The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. ● The regional development and delivery team collaborates with the U.K. and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for ensuring compliance of the company's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Germany 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Germany Chapter: Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC)**

The Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) stands as a leading engineering facility dedicated to electric mobility, integrated within an expansive international research and development (R&D) network. The center is instrumental in advancing high-end technologies that enhance premium performance driving, focusing on key areas such as Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), Electric Power Architecture (EPA), digital vehicle dynamics, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC's agile organizational structure fosters collaboration among team members across various project phases, promoting innovation and efficiency.

The operations at LTIC are organized into three primary areas:

1. **Global Development**: This team is responsible for the development of global products, particularly in digital chassis and vehicle dynamics. Their activities encompass component integration, durability testing, certification, and management of electric vehicle systems, ensuring that products meet high-performance standards.

2. **Regional Development and Delivery**: Collaborating closely with teams in the U.K. and China, this group focuses on deploying global R&D platforms while tailoring specifications to meet regional customer needs. They play a crucial role in ensuring compliance with legal and regulatory requirements, which includes conducting local legislation analyses, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity concerns.

3. **Innovation and New Platform Development**: This team is tasked with creating innovative concepts and architectures for future products. They explore emerging technologies and lead advancements in digital vehicle dynamics, positioning LTIC at the forefront of automotive innovation.

In addition to its operations in Germany, LTIC's R&D efforts extend to China, with significant activities concentrated in Wuhan, Shanghai, and Ningbo. These efforts focus on core technology innovations related to electric architecture, charging systems, cloud computing, data processing, system integration, network security, batteries, energy management, electric motors, and intelligent driving technologies. A notable initiative, Lotus Robotics, was established in China to enhance the company's intelligent driving capabilities. This initiative encompasses vehicle product development, software development, sensor technology, cloud and data management, testing, validation, and project management.

Overall, LTIC's commitment to electric mobility and advanced technology development positions it as a key player in the automotive industry, addressing both global and regional market demands while ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) is a world-class engineering facility in electric mobility. As a part of Lotus Technology's international research and development network, LTIC develops new products and solutions for a new era of premium performance driving and participates in the development of Lotus Technology's high-end technology, such as regional development for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), Electric Powertrain Architecture (EPA), new architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, vehicle hardware, and intelligent cabin systems. As of June 30, 2023, the Lotus Technology Innovation Center (LTIC) had approximately 180 full-time employees. The Lotus Technology Innovation Center adopts an agile organizational structure where its members can work in different working teams based on the focuses of various phases of the project. The Lotus Technology Innovation Center works in three main areas: • The global development team oversees global products, including, among others, digital chassis and digital vehicle dynamics. This team has assisted with component integration, durability testing, certification and homologation, as well as electric vehicle management systems. • The regional development and delivery team collaborates with the UK and China teams and is responsible for global research and development platform deployment and specification development, catering to regional customer needs. This team is also responsible for ensuring compliance of Lotus Technology's products with legal and regulatory requirements, including, among others, analyzing local legislation, managing data centers, and addressing cybersecurity matters. • The innovation and new platform development team is responsible for creating new concepts and new architectures for Lotus Technology's future product pipeline.

title: Germany 
summary: LTIC is a prominent engineering facility dedicated to electric mobility, playing a vital role in a global research and development network. The organization emphasizes the creation of innovative products and solutions that enhance premium driving performance, focusing on advanced technologies such as Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), electric powertrain architecture, digital vehicle dynamics, and intelligent cabin systems. LTIC operates with an agile structure, facilitating collaboration across various teams tailored to the specific needs of different project phases.

In parallel, China, the world's most populous country with over 1.4 billion residents, has undergone significant economic transformation, evolving into a global economic powerhouse driven by manufacturing and technology. Governed by the Communist Party, China faces challenges related to human rights and civil liberties while contributing richly to global culture and innovation. 

Within China, R&D teams in cities like Wuhan, Shanghai, and Ningbo focus on core technology innovations, including electric architecture and intelligent driving systems. Lotus Robotics, established in China, enhances this effort by specializing in intelligent driving technologies, encompassing vehicle development, software and sensor innovation, and data management. Together, these initiatives reflect China's commitment to technological advancement and its strategic role in the global economy.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022. The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology purchased a one-year convertible note with a principal amount of US$10.0 million issued by ECARX Holding Inc. Upon the listing of ECARX Holding Inc. on December 21, 2022, the note was automatically converted into Class A ordinary shares of ECARX Holding Inc. at a conversion price of US$9.50 per share. As of December 31, 2023 and 2022, the fair value of such shares was US$3.3 million and US$8.4 million, respectively. On June 24, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into the CB Purchase Agreement with Geely HK, pursuant to which Geely HK purchased from Lotus Technology the Geely Convertible Bond in two tranches in an aggregate principal amount equal to the U.S. dollar equivalent of RMB800 million through a private placement.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Related Party Transactions 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions**

During the six months ended June 30, 2024, and the corresponding periods in 2023, as well as the fiscal years ending December 31 for 2023, 2022, and 2021, the company engaged in a variety of transactions with related parties, encompassing sales of sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and R&D consulting services. The following outlines the key financial metrics and transactions:

1. **Sales and Services Provided:**
   - Services rendered to related parties totaled **US$10.7 million** in 2024, **US$5.0 million** in 2023, **US$12.6 million** in 2022, **US$8.3 million** in 2021, and **US$3.3 million** in 2020.
   - Sales to related parties amounted to **US$2.3 million** in 2024, **US$0.8 million** in 2023, **US$3.4 million** in 2022, **US$23 thousand** in 2021, and **nil** in 2020.

2. **Accounts Receivable and Contract Liabilities:**
   - Accounts receivable from related parties were **US$19.7 million** as of June 30, 2024, **US$22.4 million** at December 31, 2023, and **US$8.5 million** in 2022.
   - Contract liabilities were recorded at **US$11.0 million** as of June 30, 2024, **US$1 thousand** in 2023, and **US$8 thousand** in 2022.

3. **Prepayments and Loans:**
   - Prepayments to related parties for vehicle purchases reached **US$33.2 million** in 2024, **US$21.4 million** in 2023, and **US$6.1 million** in 2022.
   - Unsecured short-term loans to related parties totaled **nil** in 2024, **US$865 thousand** in 2023, **US$1.2 million** in 2022, and **US$2.3 million** in 2021, with interest income recognized at **US$69 thousand** in 2024 and **US$57 thousand** in 2023.
   - A significant loan agreement was executed on **March 11, 2024**, involving **US$354.0 million** over five years at an interest rate of **6.95%**.

4. **Research and Development Expenses:**
   - R&D expenses from related parties were recorded at **US$38.3 million** in 2024, **US$40.3 million** in 2023, **US$134.7 million** in 2022, **US$167.0 million** in 2021, and **US$47.4 million** in 2020.

5. **Lease Agreements and Other Transactions:**
   - Short-term lease costs incurred were **US$474 thousand** in 2024, **US$63 thousand** in 2023, and **US$765 thousand** in 2022.
   - A distribution agreement with **Lotus Cars Limited** was established on **January 31, 2023**, for **GBP18.1 million** (approximately **US$22.3 million**).

6. **Equity and Licensing Transactions:**
   - The company acquired a non-exclusive license for the **Geely automotive chassis and technology platform** for **US$288.9 million**, which was expensed as R&D.
   - An equity transfer agreement for **US$15.5 million** was completed for acquiring **Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH** in June 2022.
   - A convertible note of **US$10.0 million** was purchased from **ECARX Holding Inc.**, converting into shares valued at **US$3.3 million** in 2023.

7. **Expected Credit Loss Provision:**
   - As of June 30, 2024, the expected credit loss provision for prepayments to related parties was **US$61 thousand**.

This comprehensive summary encapsulates the significant financial metrics and transactions with related parties, highlighting the substantial amounts and agreements made during the specified periods, while ensuring clarity and coherence in the presentation of the information.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: (vi) On March 12, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into a license agreement with Zhejiang Liankong, a subsidiary of Geely Holding. Under the terms of the agreement, Lotus Technology received a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and non-sublicensable license for the electric automotive chassis and autonomous driving technology platform (the “Geely License”) with consideration of US$288,948. The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain R&D projects of Lotus Technology. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. (vii) In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses (Note 7) from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing Lotus Technology’s 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. (viii) On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology, through its subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at the consideration of US$15,512, which was settled in June 2022. The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. (ix) On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology purchased a one-year convertible note (the “Note”) with the principal of US$10,000 issued by Ecarx.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has a dedicated global team to support its R&D activities with a sizable scale and comprehensive functionalities, covering all major technological perspectives. Lotus Technology's approach to innovation demonstrates an interconnected global collaboration among highly experienced and dedicated teams in the UK, Germany, and China, each with different technological focuses. Centered around Lotus Technology's global headquarters in Wuhan, China, which focuses on cloud computing and online data processing, the company operates a software center in Shanghai focusing on global system integration and network security and a research institute in Ningbo focusing on electric architecture, charging and power systems, and intelligent driving. Lotus Technology has established two R&D centers on engineering and product design, including the Lotus Technology Creative Centre (LTCC) located in the heart of the British car industry in Coventry, UK, with a focus on automotive design and design strategy, product and brand communication, and sustainable material science, and the Lotus Technology Innovative Centre (LTIC) located in Frankfurt, Germany, with a focus on behavioral science, innovative vehicle technologies, dynamic attribute development, user interface, and regional tuning and application. Lotus Technology's research and development efforts are focusing on the development of key EV technologies while benefiting from the technological support from the Geely Holding ecosystem. Lotus Technology intends to continuously develop cutting-edge technologies, including the 800-volt EPA architecture and hardware, algorithms, and software systems to enhance the competitiveness of its vehicles. Lotus Technology also plans to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual properties and software via subscriptions to other luxury auto brands.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Worldwide Research and Development Footprint 
summary: Lotus has formed a dedicated global team to bolster its research and development (R&D) efforts, encompassing a wide array of technological expertise across its facilities in the UK, Germany, and China. The global headquarters in Wuhan focuses on cloud computing and online data processing, while the Shanghai software center specializes in system integration and network security. The Ningbo research institute is dedicated to electric architecture, charging systems, and intelligent driving technologies. Additionally, Lotus operates two main R&D centers: the Lotus Technology Creative Centre (LTCC) in Coventry, UK, which emphasizes automotive design and sustainable materials, and the Lotus Technology Innovative Centre (LTIC) in Frankfurt, Germany, which centers on behavioral science and innovative vehicle technologies.

Lotus's R&D initiatives prioritize the advancement of electric vehicle (EV) technologies, leveraging support from the Geely Holding ecosystem. The company is committed to continuous innovation, particularly in its 800-volt EPA architecture, and aims to enhance vehicle competitiveness through improvements in hardware, algorithms, and software systems. Furthermore, Lotus plans to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing intellectual properties and software to other luxury automotive brands via subscription models.

As of June 30, 2023, Lotus's global R&D team, led by industry experts, consists of 1,804 professionals with extensive knowledge in automotive engineering, software, and AI. Their diverse backgrounds contribute to a dynamic exchange of ideas, significantly enhancing Lotus's technology development, particularly in intelligence and digitalization, and driving innovation within the company.
